title: elaeagnus caspica
intro: Elaeagnus caspica, also known as the Russian olive, is a deciduous shrub native to the Caspian region, characterized by its silvery-gray foliage and small, fragrant flowers. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects. Its extracts and leaves have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, digestive problems, and respiratory infections, offering benefits like reduced inflammation, improved immune function, and enhanced wound healing. Elaeagnus caspica can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are commonly used to promote overall well-being and alleviate symptoms of various conditions.
